Hope to see you tomorrow!
Mike Ollen
Political Director, Maggie for NH

body:
Prepared by my campaign manager. Please read to see our path forward. |

Friend, I want to share this important memo prepared by my campaign manager, Kellyanne, on where we currently stand in the race for the White House and what we must do to lock in a win. If you don’t read the entire memo below, I’ve highlighted key points that you NEED to know. After reading the memo, please consider making a contribution of $100, $65, $50, $35, $15, or $5 to help us stay on track to win just 15 days from today. BEFORE donating, you’ll also be able to indicate your preference for the state we invest your contribution in. TO: dpwillis67@gmail.com FROM: Kellyanne Conway, Campaign Manager RE: Donald Trump’s Path to 270 Electoral Votes ________________________________________ Donald Trump must reach 270 electoral votes to win the presidency. We’ve outlined a map to show where we currently stand as well as the possible paths forward to win. While we are not taking any state for granted, those colored red are states that we can safely assume will vote for Donald Trump. Blue states favor Hillary. Blank states are too close to call. Over the past month, polls have shown us winning Iowa, Ohio, Maine, Florida, Nevada, and North Carolina. If we maintain our leads in those six states, we can reliably claim 266 electoral votes. Hillary can claim 193. But we’d still have 4 electoral votes to go. Polls show us close in New Hampshire, Colorado, and Pennsylvania. Winning just any one of those states would lead us to victory. We are currently executing a highly costly early voting push and get-out-the-vote operation to ensure identified Trump supporters make it to the polls before Election Day. We are also planning a $140 million-dollar ad blitz in battleground states to lead us to victory. Friend: with just 15 days left, we have a few paths to win. But we’ll need your continued support. Because you’ve been such a critical supporter, you’ll also be able to share your preference on which state we should invest your contribution in. Please make a contribution of $100, $65, $50, $35, $15, or $5 to help us stay on track to win just 15 days from today. Thank you, Donald J. Trump
CONTRIBUTE $100

subject: tonight’s debate
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Katie McGinty for Senate
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/-7204502515973357568/1165332791861182464?refcode=e20161024&refcode2=e20161024nd&amount=25)
---
Hurry --

The ***FINAL DEBATE*** of this election is just a few hours away, and we want Katie to know that we're with her when she gets on that stage. [**Can you chip in a few bucks to show you 're with her?**](https://act.myngp.com/el/-7204502515973357568/1165332791861182464?refcode=e20161024&refcode2=e20161024nd&amount=25)

Before we can reverse the damage the Obama-Clinton Democrats have inflicted on America, **we must win on Election Day -- which is now just 15 days away \-- [and I'm counting on your difference-making support to help make that happen.](http://trk.listelixr.net/c/4/?T=OTkyNjA2OTc%3AMDItYjE2Mjk4LTkyNzg5MzFlNmExZDQ5NDg5ZWJmMDE5YTAwY2Q1Mzcx%3AZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20%3AMjgyNTQ5Mjg%3AZmFsc2U%3AMQ%3A%3AaHR0cHM6Ly9kb25hdGUuZG9uYWxkanRydW1wLmNvbS9maXJzdC0xMDAtZGF5cy1vZi1hZG1pbmlzdHJhdGlvbi8_dXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249SkZDX2RpcmVjdC1hc2tfZmlyc3QtaHVuZC1kYXlzJmFkZGl0aW9uYWxbdXRtX2NvbnRlbnRdPUNDLUxZLTEwMjQxNi1maXJzdC1odW5kLWRheXMtZGp0LWNiby1qZmMtcHMmdXRtX3NvdXJjZT1wX0NDX0xZ&K=cHQnQeqPis3eWs1PVWH5QA)**

As your President, I will not waste any time, but immediately begin working to Make America Great Again.

_As soon as I enter the White House on January 20, 2017_ , **I will begin implementing my 8-Point Plan for the First 100 Days of the Trump Administration, and here 's what we'll do:**
1. On Day One, we will roll out our plan to **BUILD THE WALL** , send criminal illegal immigrants home, and finally end the open borders nightmare.

4. Work with Congress to **REPEAL** and replace the disastrous ObamaCare legislation that is ruining American healthcare.
5. Lift the radical Obama regulations on America's energy industry to **END** our dependence on foreign sources and create new jobs here at home.
6. Propose, pass, and sign into law a massive **TAX CUT** for all working Americans and unleash the jobs-creating power of our small businesses.
7. **NOMINATE** Supreme Court Justices who will uphold the Founders' Constitution and not legislate the liberal agenda from the bench, and...
8. **IMPOSE** strict new ethics rules to restore the integrity of the Office of the Secretary of State.____

subject: We need Deborah
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Deborah Ross for Senate Deborah Ross for Senate
body:
| |
---
| |
---
| Fellow Deborah supporters,

I've been watching this race carefully. I was always optimistic about Deborah's chances of winning North Carolina's Senate seat, but now I'm counting on her to win. That means I'm counting on you, too. [_**Will you step up for Deborah right now?**_](https://secure.deborahross.com/page/m/45f427dd/2cc31c44/577a131/19f994e1/2803987373/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/dre161024?refcode=dre161024_m&recur_weekly=true&amount=5")

Right now, Deborah's contest is tied up. It's one of the closest in the nation, and it's also one of the most important in the nation.

Democrats can take back the Senate and stop Republicans from delaying progress and obstructing the basic functions of government. After working with a Republican Senate for the last two years, I cannot overstate how vital it is that Democrats take the reins again.

North Carolina is the key to taking back the Senate. We need to rally around Deborah so she has the resources to win. I'm passing on the torch of progress and we need standard bearers like Deborah to carry it.

[_**There is no question that Deborah would be a strong voice for North Carolinians and middle-class families all over the country. Please give $5 or $10 to her grassroots network if you can.**_](https://secure.deborahross.com/page/m/45f427dd/2cc31c44/577a131/19f994e1/2803987373/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/dre161024?refcode=dre161024_m&recur_weekly=true&amount=5")

At the start, people discounted Deborah's campaign -- I can relate.

But Deborah wasn't discouraged. She showed toughness and a willingness to put in the hard work. She didn't rely on special interest money or 20 years as a Washington insider to get to the top of the polls.

Deborah made this a competitive race because she has a bone-deep belief in making North Carolina a better place. Her vision for this state and this country is one of progress, equality, and inclusiveness.

[_**Deborah also made this a race because she has a strong grassroots team behind her. I need her in the Senate to protect everything we 've accomplished. Can you give $5 to make sure she gets there?**_](https://secure.deborahross.com/page/m/45f427dd/2cc31c44/577a131/19f994e1/2803987373/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/dre161024?refcode=dre161024_m&recur_weekly=true&amount=5")

Thank you,

Barack |

|

This is important, friends. There is just too much on the line in North Carolina to sit this election out. I know Roy Cooper. He's a good man -- honorable, decent, and tough. The son of a school teacher who worked the family farm, the student educated in North Carolina public schools from grade school through law school. He never forgets where he comes from, and the values of the state that raised him -- never, ever put politics above the best interests of the people. That's Roy. As attorney general of North Carolina, he's taken on big banks, big pharma, payday lenders, and child predators. He's a man of integrity who always does the right thing. And that's the kind of governor this great state deserves. **[Will you chip in right now to make sure Roy wins this thing on Election Day?](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/1202a3b/2fde7c66/4328e227/156aca57/2415628895/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/cooper-20161024?utm_campaign=fnd_biden_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cooper&refcode=em16_161024_fnd_gq_biden_ful__jb&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com")** Look, being governor is a tough job. I've worked with a lot of governors on everything from keeping cops on the street and teachers in the classroom to preparing for natural disasters and rebuilding roads and bridges. I've seen governors lead the charge on raising teacher pay and expanding access to health care. And while folks are frustrated by the gridlock in Washington, they are embarrassed by governors who don't represent them. But that's what you have right now in North Carolina -- a current governor that's the face of discriminatory laws that have cost the state thousands of jobs, hundreds of millions of dollars, and untold damage to the state's reputation. Blatant attacks on voting rights that have been struck down in the court of law and ridiculed in the court of public opinion. Friends, governors set the tone in their states. And Roy Cooper will set the tone for your future. It's time for change in North Carolina. It's time to start moving forward again. And that's why I'm supporting my friend, Roy Cooper, a leader who will appeal to our hopes and not our fears. **But he needs your support, too.** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | [**$5 »**](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/1202a3b/2fde7c66/4328e227/156aca56/2415628895/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/cooper-20161024?utm_campaign=fnd_biden_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cooper&refcode=em16_161024_fnd_gq_biden_ful__jb&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&express_lane=true&amount=5")

clean_body: There is just too much on the line in North Carolina to sit this one out. There is just too much on the line in North Carolina to sit this one out. I know Roy Cooper. He's a good man -- honorable, decent, and tough. | "" This is important, friends. There is just too much on the line in North Carolina to sit this election out. I know Roy Cooper. He's a good man -- honorable, decent, and tough. The son of a school teacher who worked the family farm, the student educated in North Carolina public schools from grade school through law school. He never forgets where he comes from, and the values of the state that raised him -- never, ever put politics above the best interests of the people. That's Roy. As attorney general of North Carolina, he's taken on big banks, big pharma, payday lenders, and child predators. He's a man of integrity who always does the right thing. And that's the kind of governor this great state deserves. "" Look, being governor is a tough job. I've worked with a lot of governors on everything from keeping cops on the street and teachers in the classroom to preparing for natural disasters and rebuilding roads and bridges. I've seen governors lead the charge on raising teacher pay and expanding access to health care. And while folks are frustrated by the gridlock in Washington, they are embarrassed by governors who don't represent them. But that's what you have right now in North Carolina -- a current governor that's the face of discriminatory laws that have cost the state thousands of jobs, hundreds of millions of dollars, and untold damage to the state's reputation. Blatant attacks on voting rights that have been struck down in the court of law and ridiculed in the court of public opinion. Friends, governors set the tone in their states. And Roy Cooper will set the tone for your future. It's time for change in North Carolina. It's time to start moving forward again. And that's why I'm supporting my friend, Roy Cooper, a leader who will appeal to our hopes and not our fears. But he needs your support, too. Friend, I want to share this important memo prepared by my campaign manager, Kellyanne, on where we currently stand in the race for the White House and what we must do to lock in a win. If you don’t read the entire memo below, I’ve highlighted key points that you NEED to know. After reading the memo, please consider making a contribution of $100, $65, $50, $35, $15, or $5 to help us stay on track to win just 15 days from today. We’ve outlined a map to show where we currently stand as well as the possible paths forward to win. While we are not taking any state for granted, those colored red are states that we can safely assume will vote for Donald Trump. Blue states favor Hillary. Blank states are too close to call. Over the past month, polls have shown us winning Iowa, Ohio, Maine, Florida, Nevada, and North Carolina. If we maintain our leads in those six states, we can reliably claim 266 electoral votes. Hillary can claim 193. But we’d still have 4 electoral votes to go. Polls show us close in New Hampshire, Colorado, and Pennsylvania. Winning just any one of those states would lead us to victory. We are currently executing a highly costly early voting push and get-out-the-vote operation to ensure identified Trump supporters make it to the polls before Election Day. We are also planning a $140 million-dollar ad blitz in battleground states to lead us to victory. Friend: with just 15 days left, we have a few paths to win. But we’ll need your continued support. Because you’ve been such a critical supporter, you’ll also be able to share your preference on which state we should invest your contribution in. Please make a contribution of $100, $65, $50, $35, $15, or $5 to help us stay on track to win just 15 days from today. Thank you, Donald J. Trump

| **For Immediate Release:**
Monday, October 24, 2016

**Contact:** Ethan Latour
[ethan@philscott.org](mailto:ethan@philscott.org)
802-595-3776
**Graded on Votes to Support Jobs, Minter Gets an F
_Phil Scott Gets an A_**
Middlesex, Vt. – Legislative report cards from 2007-2011 show Sue Minter received a grade of F and only voted for pro-jobs, pro-growth policies only 25% of the time, while Phil Scott earned an A.

“On numerous occasions, when Sue Minter had to choose between voting for job creation and working families, Minter voted against them,” said Scott campaign spokeswoman Brittney Wilson. “Minter voted against property tax relief; to shift more costs onto health insurance premiums; and raise $26 million in taxes while slashing workforce-training funds.”

**Minter was also a sponsor of two new gas taxes, a mileage tax and an income tax increase on filers making more than $47,000 per year.”**

Over the same period Phil Scott earned an A, voting to support small businesses and working families 91% of the time — among the strongest pro-growth records in the Senate. The Vermont Chamber of Commerce published the report cards.

“Unlike his opponent, Phil fought to create jobs by supporting a Research and Development Tax Credit, supporting economic growth incentives, and voting against millions of dollars worth of tax increases that would have directly hurt small businesses and their employees,” Wilson said. “He supported Vermont workers by voting for investments in workforce development, voting to tie minimum wage increases to the cost of living, and voting for the 2010 Jobs Bill which increased funding for the Vermont Economic Development Authority and the Vermont Training Program.”

**Wilson said the choice Vermonters have in this election is clear and based on the candidates’ records, not just their rhetoric.**

“Phil Scott has a plan – and a voting record – that demonstrates a clear commitment to growing the economy and making Vermont more affordable for families and businesses,” Wilson said. “Sue Minter’s record is marked by higher property taxes, higher health insurance costs, and millions in higher income taxes—all while cutting workforce-training funds. That’s not the record of someone who is committed to make Vermont more affordable for working families or job creators – it’s the record of someone whose votes help to create Vermont’s crisis of affordability.”

clean_body: | | | | | | | | | | ---|--- | | | ---|--- | | | ---|--- | For Immediate Release: Monday, October 24, 2016 Contact: Ethan Latour mailto:ethan@philscott.org 802-595-3776 Graded on Votes to Support Jobs, Minter Gets an F Phil Scott Gets an A Middlesex, Vt. – Legislative report cards from 2007-2011 show Sue Minter received a grade of F and only voted for pro-jobs, pro-growth policies only 25% of the time, while Phil Scott earned an A. “On numerous occasions, when Sue Minter had to choose between voting for job creation and working families, Minter voted against them,” said Scott campaign spokeswoman Brittney Wilson. “Minter voted against property tax relief; to shift more costs onto health insurance premiums; and raise $26 million in taxes while slashing workforce-training funds.” Minter was also a sponsor of two new gas taxes, a mileage tax and an income tax increase on filers making more than $47,000 per year.” Over the same period Phil Scott earned an A, voting to support small businesses and working families 91% of the time — among the strongest pro-growth records in the Senate. The Vermont Chamber of Commerce published the report cards. “Unlike his opponent, Phil fought to create jobs by supporting a Research and Development Tax Credit, supporting economic growth incentives, and voting against millions of dollars worth of tax increases that would have directly hurt small businesses and their employees,” Wilson said. “He supported Vermont workers by voting for investments in workforce development, voting to tie minimum wage increases to the cost of living, and voting for the 2010 Jobs Bill which increased funding for the Vermont Economic Development Authority and the Vermont Training Program.” Wilson said the choice Vermonters have in this election is clear and based on the candidates’ records, not just their rhetoric. “Phil Scott has a plan – and a voting record – that demonstrates a clear commitment to growing the economy and making Vermont more affordable for families and businesses,” Wilson said. “Sue Minter’s record is marked by higher property taxes, higher health insurance costs, and millions in higher income taxes—all while cutting workforce-training funds. That’s not the record of someone who is committed to make Vermont more affordable for working families or job creators – it’s the record of someone whose votes help to create Vermont’s crisis of affordability.” | | | | | | | | | | | | Paid for by Phil Scott For Vermont 2016 46 Three Mile Bridge Rd. | Friends,

Just wanted to send you a quick note to make sure you saw President Obama's email from earlier.

I am so honored to have him on our team. The President knows what's at stake here in North Carolina, and we don't have a lot of time left.

[_**Will you join Obama and help us win on November 8th? Give $5 or $10 right now >>**_](https://secure.deborahross.com/page/m/45f427df/2cc31c44/57a2df8/19f9aa84/23523339/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/dre161024pm?refcode=dre161024pm_m&recur_weekly=true&amount=5")

Thanks again for your support,

Deborah \---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: Barack Obama
Subject: We need Deborah Fellow Deborah supporters,

I've been watching this race carefully. I was always optimistic about Deborah's chances of winning North Carolina's Senate seat, but now I'm counting on her to win. That means I'm counting on you, too. [_**Will you step up for Deborah right now?**_](https://secure.deborahross.com/page/m/45f427df/2cc31c44/57a2df8/19f9aa84/23523339/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/dre161024pm?refcode=dre161024pm_m&recur_weekly=true&amount=5")

Right now, Deborah's contest is tied up. It's one of the closest in the nation, and it's also one of the most important in the nation.

Democrats can take back the Senate and stop Republicans from delaying progress and obstructing the basic functions of government. After working with a Republican Senate for the last two years, I cannot overstate how vital it is that Democrats take the reins again.

North Carolina is the key to taking back the Senate. We need to rally around Deborah so she has the resources to win. I'm passing on the torch of progress and we need standard bearers like Deborah to carry it.

[_**There is no question that Deborah would be a strong voice for North Carolinians and middle-class families all over the country. Please give $5 or $10 to her grassroots network if you can.**_](https://secure.deborahross.com/page/m/45f427df/2cc31c44/57a2df8/19f9aa84/23523339/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/dre161024pm?refcode=dre161024pm_m&recur_weekly=true&amount=5")

At the start, people discounted Deborah's campaign -- I can relate.

But Deborah wasn't discouraged. She showed toughness and a willingness to put in the hard work. She didn't rely on special interest money or 20 years as a Washington insider to get to the top of the polls.

Deborah made this a competitive race because she has a bone-deep belief in making North Carolina a better place. Her vision for this state and this country is one of progress, equality, and inclusiveness.

[_**Deborah also made this a race because she has a strong grassroots team behind her. I need her in the Senate to protect everything we 've accomplished. Can you give $5 to make sure she gets there?**_](https://secure.deborahross.com/page/m/45f427df/2cc31c44/57a2df8/19f9aa84/23523339/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/dre161024pm?refcode=dre161024pm_m&recur_weekly=true&amount=5")

Thank you,

Barack |

name: Bernie Sanders
subject: Urgent situations
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Bernie Sanders
body:
#
Sisters and Brothers,
Election Day is in two weeks, and we cannot allow the Koch brothers and other billionaires to buy elections throughout the country. Let's stand together and elect strong progressives who are fighting for real change - fighting to stand up for working families, the elderly, the children, the sick and the poor. Let's support candidates who are prepared to take on the fossil fuel industry and transform our energy system away from coal and oil into energy efficiency and sustainable energy.
Our candidates are working hard and, in many cases, the polls show the races are very close. It is likely that the outcome will be decided by a few votes - one way or the other. Let's make sure that our candidates are on top.
I want to tell you about these candidates, their close races, and the billionaires trying to defeat them. But if you can contribute right now, [**please split a $3 contribution between the campaigns of Jane Kim, Sue Minter, David Zuckerman, Proposition 61 in California, and Eloise Reyes.**](https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/billionaires-buying-elections?refcode=em161024-full "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/billionaires-buying-elections?refcode=em161024-full")
In California, Jane Kim - one of the first candidates I endorsed, and who won her incredibly close primary because of your support - is facing an outrageous amount of outside spending in her race for state senate. **Billionaire-funded groups have spent $500,000 attacking her in just the last three weeks** , with more sure to come. Jane is a true progressive, and she can defeat the big money interests if we are prepared to stand behind her.
In my home state of Vermont, Sue Minter is the Democratic nominee for Governor. She will be a strong leader in making higher education tuition free for all, and in the struggle against climate change. **But a new poll shows she is one point behind the Republican nominee, who is receiving strong support from billionaires around the country.** In my view her running-mate **David Zuckerman** , a family farmer and food activist, will be the most progressive Lieutenant Governor in the country. He is an outstanding member of the state senate who has taken on powerful special interests throughout his career.
Proposition 61 in California would lower prescription drug prices and challenge the price gouging of Big Pharma. As you might imagine, drug companies don't like it very much. **They are spending more than $100 million to defeat this initiative, and just added another $22 million last week.** A victory in California against the greed of the pharmaceutical industry is a victory for all of us.
Big oil companies want to defeat Eloise Reyes in her race for state legislature in California. **Chevron has donated millions of dollars to outside groups trying to defeat Reyes** because they know she would hold them accountable in the fight against climate change.
With just 15 days until the election, we need your support to expand our political revolution. Great candidates are being overwhelmed with huge contributions from the Koch brothers and other billionaires. Let's fight back and help our candidates win.
[**Please split a $3 contribution between Jane Kim, Sue Minter, David Zuckerman, Proposition 61 in California, and Eloise Reyes.**](https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/billionaires-buying-elections?refcode=em161024-full "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/billionaires-buying-elections?refcode=em161024-full")
In solidarity,
Bernie Sanders

clean_body: Sisters and Brothers, Election Day is in two weeks, and we cannot allow the Koch brothers and other billionaires to buy elections throughout the country. Let's stand together and elect strong progressives who are fighting for real change - fighting to stand up for working families, the elderly, the children, the sick and the poor. Let's support candidates who are prepared to take on the fossil fuel industry and transform our energy system away from coal and oil into energy efficiency and sustainable energy. Our candidates are working hard and, in many cases, the polls show the races are very close. It is likely that the outcome will be decided by a few votes - one way or the other. Let's make sure that our candidates are on top. I want to tell you about these candidates, their close races, and the billionaires trying to defeat them. But if you can contribute right now, "" In California, Jane Kim - one of the first candidates I endorsed, and who won her incredibly close primary because of your support - is facing an outrageous amount of outside spending in her race for state senate. Billionaire-funded groups have spent $500,000 attacking her in just the last three weeks , with more sure to come. Jane is a true progressive, and she can defeat the big money interests if we are prepared to stand behind her. In my home state of Vermont, Sue Minter is the Democratic nominee for Governor. She will be a strong leader in making higher education tuition free for all, and in the struggle against climate change. But a new poll shows she is one point behind the Republican nominee, who is receiving strong support from billionaires around the country. In my view her running-mate David Zuckerman , a family farmer and food activist, will be the most progressive Lieutenant Governor in the country. He is an outstanding member of the state senate who has taken on powerful special interests throughout his career. Proposition 61 in California would lower prescription drug prices and challenge the price gouging of Big Pharma. As you might imagine, drug companies don't like it very much. They are spending more than $100 million to defeat this initiative, and just added another $22 million last week. A victory in California against the greed of the pharmaceutical industry is a victory for all of us. Big oil companies want to defeat Eloise Reyes in her race for state legislature in California. Chevron has donated millions of dollars to outside groups trying to defeat Reyes because they know she would hold them accountable in the fight against climate change. With just 15 days until the election, we need your support to expand our political revolution. Great candidates are being overwhelmed with huge contributions from the Koch brothers and other billionaires. Let's fight back and help our candidates win. "" In solidarity, Bernie Sanders "" Paid for by Friends of Bernie Sanders PO BOX 391, Burlington, VT 05402

subject: YOU stepped up
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:
We need to make sure people go to the polls to have their voices heard during the week of Early Voting, which starts this Thursday, and on Election Day, November 8th. |
[](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91a97/70cdc2ba/47284beb/397b659/111097083/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/cvh-keep-fighting?refcode=em092816_2&amount=25.00&recurring=12&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=em102016&source=em102016") Friend, many thanks for all that you've done for this campaign. The strong support from you and many others has been the backbone of Chris' campaign for U.S. Senate and you came through again for last week's final FEC deadline.

Thanks to your support we are in a strong position as we head into the final two weeks of this campaign. But this has been a crazy political year and we can't take anything for grantedc!

We need to make sure people go to the polls to have their voices heard during the week of Early Voting, which starts this Thursday, and on Election Day, November 8th.

There are still thousands of voters undecided about who they plan to vote for in the Maryland Senate race -- so in the final weeks of this campaign we must reach out to as many of these voters as possible. **[Please sign up now to be a Team Van Hollen volunteer and help with this final GOTV effort.](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91a97/70cdc2ba/47284beb/397b656/111097083/VEsE/ "http://action.vanhollen.org/page/s/volunteer-for-chris?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=2+-+Please+sign+up+now+to+be+a+Team+Van+Holl&utm_campaign=em102016&source=em102016")** [I'll volunteer for Chris](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91a97/70cdc2ba/47284beb/397b657/111097083/VEsF/ "http://action.vanhollen.org/page/s/volunteer-for-chris?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=3+-+Irsquoll+volunteer+for+Chris&utm_campaign=em102016&source=em102016")

Pat Murray

subject: These House women need us now >>
disclaimer_text: Paid for by EMILY's List [www
body:
| [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c0b13/24c69fd9/ca35cd2/18ea0248/3596220913/VEsH/ "https://www.emilyslist.org/donate?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WPEFCV65N_digital_email_20161024_House_n-all&source=WPEFCV65N_digital_email_20161024_House_n-all")

| | Hi there,

Three House women need us right now and cannot wait.

**Nanette Barrag an** is running in California's 44th Congressional District. Nanette was outraised by her opponent in the last fundraising quarter, and we need to help her make up the difference.

**Jacky Rosen** has a tough battle in Nevada's Third Congressional District that _Cook Political Report_ rates as a toss-up. Republicans have committed to giving her opponent extra support, and Jacky needs a boost now if she's going to win this.

And **Kim Myers** is running for an open seat in NY-22. Republicans have held this seat for the past three years, and they're going to do all they can to hold on to it. Kim needs us to counter the resources they're pouring into this race as we speak.

**[Donate $3 or more today to help elect Nanette, Jacky, and Kim clean up the House. 100% of your donation will go directly to their campaigns.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c0b13/24c69fd9/ca35cd2/18ea0249/3596220913/VEsE/ "https://www.emilyslist.org/donate/page/20161024_email_House?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Donate+3+or+more+today+to+help+elect+Nan&utm_campaign=WPEFCV65N_digital_email_20161024_House_n-all&source=WPEFCV65N_digital_email_20161024_House_n-all")**

Nanette's been endorsed by the _Los Angeles Times_ , but her opponent is listed as the front-runner. Nanette is the better candidate -- she fought tirelessly for the environment on the Hermosa Beach City Council, and she'll be a champion for women and families in her district. We know she can pull into the lead -- that's why we have to rush support to her now.

In their endorsement of Jacky the _Las Vegas Sun_ wrote of her opponent, "We don't trust Tarkanian." We don't either. He's a Trump supporter -- still -- and is staunchly anti-choice. Jacky will stand up for women's health care and fight for progressive policies, but she needs us to back her up now if she's going to win.

Kim has spent decades fighting for families in upstate New York, including 18 years on her local board of education. We know she's the right choice in this race, but Kim was tied with her Republican opponent in the polls last month, and a third-party candidate is pulling over 20 percent of voters. This is going to be a _tight_ race to the finish, and Kim can't win alone.

**Help Nanette, Jacky, and Kim in these critical last days of their races. 100% of your donation will go directly to their campaigns:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Help-Nanette-Jacky-And-Kim](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c0b13/24c69fd9/ca35cd2/18ea024a/3596220913/VEsF/ "https://www.emilyslist.org/donate/page/20161024_email_House?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgHelpNanetteJacky&utm_campaign=WPEFCV65N_digital_email_20161024_House_n-all&source=WPEFCV65N_digital_email_20161024_House_n-all")**

Thank you,

Lucinda Guinn
Vice President of Campaigns, EMILY's List

| Once President Obama reached out to you this morning, I thought we would be closer to our grassroots goal. We're still behind.

We're $15,000 short as of right now, and our deadline is in 6 hours. [_**Can you rush in $5 right now?**_](https://secure.deborahross.com/page/m/45f427d1/2cc31c44/57eb84e/19f9a93d/4199027120/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/dre161024pm2?refcode=dre161024pm2_m&recur_weekly=true&amount=5")

This is exactly why we have to work harder than Senator Burr. Even though we have the support of everyday North Carolinians, Senator Burr has millionaire backers who can drown us out by writing seven-figure checks.

[_**We can overcome special interest spending. We 've done it throughout this campaign. We just can't let up. Our deadline is 6 hours away.
